concept.domains: (topic concept) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c concept oceConcept-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
concept.title: 15 Extended explanation SDS test 15-6-024 Media step accuracy

abstract.p: The principle goal of this test is to diagnose the media step quality (mainly banding related print quality artifacts) but it can also be used as "swiss knife" tool in case of need.

note.p: This test was introduced before the introduction of the advanced technical views on media step performance. This technical view provides more accurate information than this test.

abstract.p: The test prints page wide measure makers (in a 4-pass mode) on a 1.2m long print. During the test, the markers are read and the media step error is calculated. Precondition is that the media is calibrated up-front otherwise there is no correction model.
Since the media transport is influenced by different machine geometry "obstacles" the measurements are segmented in 6 equal (200mm) zones.

section.title: Description

li.p: Anywhere, from leading edge and beyond
In each output configuration with winding disabled
The Automatic Advance Correction (AAC) is disabled in this test.
The step correction is always 0, regardless of the settings in the RIP.
Print surface temperature and vacuum settings are taken from the media related RIP settings.
the average media (sum step) error per segment [um]

section.p: On fresh reference media, the media step accuracy should meet the following targets:

li.p: the average media (sum step) error per segment [um] +/- 33 um. Displayed in tab named “”Segment AverageErrors””.
the maximum-minimum (range) error per segment [um] < 50 um. Displayed in tab named “SegmentErrorRanges”.

p.b: This results in a banding free print.

section.title: IMPORTANT when testing with non-reference media !

li.p: For all media, the same targets apply. However, achieving these target cannot be guaranteed, since the possibility exists that media types or batches exist which have runnability properties , less compatible with the media handling. This can still result in acceptable print quality for the customer.
In case the visual results of the test are not satisfactory, but the reported results are within limits, the media handling is OK.

Optimized RIP settings (such as a step size offset and/or non-default print strategy) can eliminate these artefacts. Since the SDS test only offers printing under default conditions, banding artefact may visible in the SDS test output, which are not seen in customer print made with optimized RIP settings.
